output:
CVE-2021-24862 is a high-severity SQL Injection vulnerability affecting the RegistrationMagic WordPress plugin versions prior to 5.0.1.6. The vulnerability arises because the rm_chronos_ajax AJAX action fails to properly escape user input before using it in SQL statements, allowing an attacker to manipulate the SQL query.

This vulnerability is categorized under CWE-89, which pertains to improperly neutralized special elements in SQL commands. Exploitation of this flaw can lead to unauthorized access to the database and potentially allow an attacker to execute arbitrary SQL commands.

The affected software includes specific versions of the RegistrationMagic plugin for WordPress. To mitigate this vulnerability, users should upgrade to version 5.0.1.6 or later. Additionally, implementing defense-in-depth strategies such as input validation and parameterized queries will help prevent similar vulnerabilities.

This CVE is associated with MITRE ATT&CK Technique T1190, which involves exploiting vulnerable public-facing applications. Mitigation techniques include ensuring regular updates and employing web application firewalls.
